question: How old is India?

theme.label: People and demography
readerQuestions: Is the trend rising, falling, or slowing?
What is the age or sex structure behind the total?
What does this change for jobs, schools, care, and families?

visualPlan.title: Median age of population
visualPlan.recommendedVisualization: mobile horizontal bars or selected multi-line chart
visualPlan.mobileGuidance: Prefer horizontal bars, share strips, or at most three lines; avoid dense tables.
visualPlan.whyThisMatters: This is the chart that answers the page question most directly.

visualPlan.title: Population by 5-year age groups and sex
visualPlan.recommendedVisualization: population pyramid plus horizontal age-band bars
visualPlan.mobileGuidance: Use a full-width pyramid on desktop; on mobile keep labels short and preserve left/right symmetry.
visualPlan.whyThisMatters: This chart adds context, structure, change, or a caveat to the headline answer.

visualPlan.title: Population ages 0-14
visualPlan.recommendedVisualization: single-series line chart with latest value and first-to-latest change
visualPlan.mobileGuidance: Use a line chart with large labels, sparse ticks, and a short reader caption.
visualPlan.whyThisMatters: This chart adds context, structure, change, or a caveat to the headline answer.

plannedCharts.title: India's median age, 2000 to 2030
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The single clearest number for how old the country is.

plannedCharts.title: Age pyramid, 2025
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The full age-and-sex shape behind a single median number.

plannedCharts.title: How India's age mix has shifted
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Ageing is really three shares moving at once.

plannedCharts.title: Age structure today
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The population split into life stages right now.

plannedCharts.title: Old-age dependency ratio
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: How many older adults each 100 working-age adults support.

plannedCharts.title: Child dependency ratio
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The youth side of the dependency picture.

plannedCharts.title: Life expectancy at birth
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Longer lives are half of why a population ages.

plannedCharts.title: Life expectancy at 60
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: How many more years someone who reaches 60 can expect.

sourceSummaries.title: Life expectancy by sex at birth

selectionRules: Start from the page question, not from the indicator name.
Choose the smallest set of visuals that explains scale, trend, structure, comparison, and caveat.
Prefer mobile-friendly charts: line charts with sparse ticks, horizontal bars, share strips, pyramids only when essential, and no dense tables.
Use one strong pull quote only when the evidence supports it with a number.
Add glossary blocks for technical terms that normal readers may not know.
caveats: Use only figures present in lockedNumbers or sourceSummaries.
If a related theme indicator is present, you may use it for context, but do not pretend it is the main answer.
If source coverage is thin, say so directly.
Do not compare states, districts, or cities unless the evidence packet contains those geographies.
forbiddenClaims: Do not infer causality from a trend unless the evidence explicitly supports it.
Do not cite a source that is not present in sourceSummaries.
